title: ursinia montana subsp. tenuiloba
intro: Ursinia montana subsp. tenuiloba, also known as the Mountain daisy or Small-leaved Ursinia, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Ursinia montana subsp. tenuiloba include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antibacterial agent, making it a valuable herb for treating various health issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using it as a topical application to benefit from its medicinal properties.
